mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity risk premiums lower and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite UNRATE < 6.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with rising housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion. When both series rise simultaneously, corporate profit margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent alongside rising retail sales indicates resilient consumer demand. This environment supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are flat or negative when GDPC1 growth > 2.0% and RSAFS rises, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 255, inflation expectations embed into longer-term rates, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of the periods following CPI > 255, or the negative correlation between inflation surprises and SPX returns reverses sign over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects immediate capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which directly supports near-term corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls coupled with increasing housing starts indicate sustained household formation and consumption. This dual expansion reinforces earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a positive risk premium over a 2-year rolling window, or the multivariate regression shows a negative coefficient for the interaction term with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter, demand weakness triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances despite GDP growth < 1.0%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
